body_txt: There is a curious phenomenon in commercials in which edible animals or the post-prepared food and drink is given intelligence and the power of speech. And it wants humans to eat it. Or at least, others of its kind. The title comes from a line of dialogue in Douglas Adams ' The Restaurant at the End of the Universe . The titular Restaurant serves a "Dish of the Day" which is a live, talking cow . The Dish calmly explains that this is a cruelty-free restaurant and he is looking forward to being eaten. The protagonist Arthur reacts with horror, but everyone else accepts this. Ironically, several science-fiction works written since have played this idea straight, often crossed with I'm a Humanitarian for maximum Nightmare Fuel . There is, naturally, a great deal of cross-over with Carnivore Confusion . May also overlap with Fridge Horror , and more often than not with Cargo Envy . Can be horrifying, sick or somewhat funny depending on the person. Can sometimes be justified by using fruit, which quite logically should want to be eaten so that its seeds will be spread. Comes in a few variations: Compare Black Comedy Cannibalism , Let's All Go to the Parody , Meat-O-Vision , and Stewed Alive . Contrast You Won't Like How I Taste where the edible person doesn't want to be eaten. Contains aspects of Tools of Sapience . Often overlaps with Restaurant Mascot . Also compare Aren't You Going to Ravish Me? for a variant that has nothing to do with food. If the animal starts having second thoughts, does that mean it's having a Meal Realization ? Sometimes overlaps with Reverse Psycho (an ad begs you to buy the product by telling you not to).   An animal or food product is advertising itself as food. Shown in the page image.                        An animal or food product is shown eating others of its own kind, and advertising them as food.               A variation of this trope, where some other animal or food is trying to get you to eat the food advertised, presumably so you don't eat it .       An animal or food doesn't want to be eaten, and is running from you, its predator. Usually played for Black Comedy .             An inferior food product wants to be eaten, but can't, because it's not as good as the product being advertised.
